description.content:
List available games with optional filtering, search, and pagination.
Results are scoped to the games enabled for the calling operator's
configured providers and selections.

query.description: Filter by the provider's own game identifier.
Sort field; prefix with `-` for descending.

Filter to games playable in this ISO-4217 currency. A game matches
when the currency is in its effective `supported_currencies` (the
game's own list if set, else the provider default). Use this to build
a storefront that never shows a player a game their wallet currency
cannot open.

response.name: Page of games.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

header.description: Echoes the active environment for this key.
Rate-limit ceiling for this key on this endpoint family.
Calls remaining in the current window.
Unix timestamp when the current rate-limit window resets.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

List the providers registered on the platform with their
`supported_currencies` and `blocked_countries` defaults. Use this to learn
provider-level currency support and blocked countries before fetching
games, so you can filter your storefront and avoid launch-time dead-ends.
Game-level values (`GET /v1/games`) override these when a game declares
its own list.

response.name: The provider registry.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Missing, invalid, or revoked API key.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Create a real-money game session. The player is redirected to the
returned `game_url` to begin playing. The URL is single-use — open it
once and do not cache or reload it; create the session immediately
before redirecting.

Requires `Idempotency-Key` header. Retries with the same key within
24 hours return the cached response.

header.description:
(Required) Unique key for idempotent retry semantics. Retries within 24 hours
return the cached response. Same key with a different body returns
409 Conflict.

response.name: Session created.

response.name:
Game or player not eligible. The `error.code` distinguishes:
`E2002` game not enabled for the operator, `E2003` game blocked in the
player's `country` (jurisdiction), `E3004` player self-excluded, or
`operator_settings_not_configured` when the operator has no wallet
callback configured for the provider.

description.content:
Launch a demo game session. No real-money wallet involvement; no bet
or win callbacks emitted. Useful for sandbox testing or
prospect-facing free-play surfaces. Demo sessions are non-financial
and do not require an `Idempotency-Key` header.

List bet and win transactions for a session or player, with optional
time-range filtering and pagination. Used for reconciliation against
your wallet ledger.

response.name: Page of transactions.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Missing, invalid, or revoked API key.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Issue free rounds (free spins) to a player on one or more games. `count`
is **per game** — `games: [A, B]` with `count: 10` issues 10 rounds on
each (20 total). Not tied to a session; every game must support free
rounds and passes the same jurisdiction check as a launch.

Requires the `write` scope and an `Idempotency-Key` header (same key +
same body replays the cached result; same key + different body → 409).
Free rounds must be enabled for the provider before issuance succeeds.

header.description:
(Required) Unique key for idempotent retry semantics. Retries within 24 hours
return the cached response. Same key with a different body returns
409 Conflict.

variable.description: Your operator API key from app.aggregator.gg/api-keys. Replace before sending requests.
Auto-generated unique key for retryable POSTs. Postman regenerates per-request because $randomUUID is a dynamic variable.

description.content:
B2B game aggregation platform. Server-to-server REST API for operators to
browse the game catalog, launch real-money and demo sessions, and query
transactions.

**Authentication**: Bearer token via `Authorization` header. All
operator API keys are prefixed `sk_live_*`. Welcome credits make
pre-launch testing safe — see docs.aggregator.gg/get-started/environments/.

**Versioning**: Hybrid path-version (`/v1/`) plus `Aggregator-Version`
header for additive minor revisions. See
https://docs.aggregator.gg/resources/versioning/.

**Idempotency**: POST endpoints touching financial state require an
`Idempotency-Key` header. Cached responses replay for 24 hours.

This spec is the source of truth. CI rejects route changes that drift
from this file via a schemathesis conformance test.


Contact Support:
 Name: Aggregator API support
